channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Catholic community in Utah 'united in prayer' for the health of Pope Francis
item.description: The pope showed some slight improvements in recent tests, and as he continues to recover, members of the Catholic faith around the world — including in Utah — continue to pray for his health.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Musk’s DOGE moves will ‘turn out more Democrats than Obama': Carville
item.description: Carville told NewsNation that the Tesla billionaire will be "the greatest generator of Democratic turnout, ever, more than Barack Obama.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KREX 5

item.title: Supreme Court declines to take up challenge to Tennessee law restricting drag shows
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to hear a challenge to a Tennessee law restricting some drag performances, allowing the first-in-the-nation law to remain largely intact.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KREX 5

item.title: Inmate death reported as illegal prison guard strike stretches into 2nd week
item.description: COXSACKIE, N.Y. (AP) — A wildcat strike by guards at New York's prisons, now in its second week, is fueling fears over deteriorating conditions behind bars.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, CEDAR ATTANASIO and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Funeral set for an officer killed while responding to a gunman in a Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: A funeral service will be held later this week for a Pennsylvania police officer who was killed last weekend while responding to a gunman inside a hospital intensive care unit.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: McConnell suggests Trump has ‘gross misunderstanding’ of Ukraine talks
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) on Monday suggested that President Trump’s refusal to blame Russia as the sole aggressor who provoked the war in Ukraine “reflects a gross misunderstanding of the nature of negotiations and leverage.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Delta flight returns to Atlanta after possible smoke reported in flightdeck
item.description: A Delta flight returned to Atlanta on Monday after the crew observed a “haze” in the aircraft shortly after takeoff, the airline said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 3 boaters stuck at sea for 9 hours after their boat capsizes
item.description: Coast Guard crews have rescued three boaters in Florida after their boat flipped over and they were stuck at sea for nine hours.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: 11 dead after supplemental shakes linked to listeria cases in 21 states, including Florida
item.description: Frozen shakes sold to nursing homes and other institutions are being recalled after a listeria outbreak in 21 states that has caused 11 deaths, according to the Federal Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Johnston, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jury selection begins for trial of suburban Chicago July 4 parade mass shooting suspect
item.description: Jury selection is underway for the trial of the man accused of opening fire on a suburban Chicago Independence Day parade and killing seven people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US Coast Guard rescues 3 after boat capsizes off Florida coast: video
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard shared footage from a weekend rescue after three overdue boaters were stranded off the coast of Florida when their boat capsized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bell takes only lead in OT to edge Hocevar, Larson in another thrilling NASCAR finish in Atlanta
item.description: Christopher Bell celebrated a drought-ending victory for himself and Joe Gibbs Racing after a dramatic comeback in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key U.S. agencies, including the FBI, State Department and the Pentagon, have instructed their employees not to comply with cost-cutting chief Elon Musk's latest demand that federal workers explain what they accomplished last week — or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Lakers snap Nuggets' 9-game winning streak behind Luka Doncic's best game yet for LA
item.description: Luka Doncic scored 32 points in his best game by far with the Lakers, leading Los Angeles to a 123-100 runaway win at Denver on Saturday night that snapped the Nuggets' nine-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Memorial grows for Pennsylvania officer killed in hospital shooting
item.description: A memorial is growing outside the police department in West York, a borough outside of York, Pennsylvania, after officer Andrew Duarte was killed in the line of duty on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, ABC 4

item.title: What to look for when buying a used car
item.description: With used car prices still at historic highs, it’s more important than ever before to be confident in your used car purchase.
item.dc:creator: iSeeCars, Fox 5

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Fox 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Fox 5

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump Education pick Linda McMahon clears committee, heads to full Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon, President Trump’s pick for Education secretary, passed the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee on Thursday morning, sending her confirmation to the full Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9
